	<@d :: "d">
	<@e :: "">
		"e"
	<@f :: "">
		"f"
```

Comment:
- `<+<` is not `AddRightEdgesIntoFirstLeftEdgeAndSetStoreForSubGraphAsIsInLeftVertex` as the `init` disapeared
- `<<<` is not `AddRightEdgesIntoFirstLeftEdgeAndSetStoreForSubGraphIncludingLinksAsIsInLeftVertex ` as the `init` disapeared

- `<<<` is not `AddRightEdgesIntoFirstLeftEdgeAndSetStoreForSubGraphIncludingLinksAsIsInLeftVertex ` as the `copy` disapeared

	<@b :: "copy">
	<@b :: "b">
	<@c :: "copy">
		"c"
	<@d :: "d">
	<@e :: "">
		"e"
	<@f :: "">
		"f"
```

Comment:
- `<+<` is not `AddRightEdgesIntoFirstLeftEdgeAndSetStoreForSubGraphAsIsInLeftVertex` as the `copy` disapeared
- `<<<` is not `AddRightEdgesIntoFirstLeftEdgeAndSetStoreForSubGraphIncludingLinksAsIsInLeftVertex ` as the `copy` disapeared

		attribute "e" @VertexType

			b += "b"
			c +< "c"

			e <+< "e"
			f <<< "f"

			this\a = "a"
			this\b += "b"

## Initialized attribute usage with `this` two times

Code:

```-0
"EXAMPLE"
	variable "o" @tst
	class "tst"
		attribute "a" @VertexType
		attribute "b" @VertexType
		attribute "c" @VertexType
		attribute "d" @VertexType
		attribute "e" @VertexType
		attribute "f" @VertexType
		method "doTest" ()
			this\a = "init"
			this\b = "init"
			this\c = "init"
			this\d = "init"
			this\e = "init"
			this\f = "init"
			this\a = "a"
			this\b += "b"
			this\c +< "c"
			this\d <- "d"
			this\e <+< "e"
			this\f <<< "f"
	o = new @@tst[]
	o.doTest[]
```

Result:
```-0

	<@b :: "init">
	<@c :: "init">
		"c"
	<@d :: "d">
	<@e :: "">
		"e"
	<@f :: "">
		"f"
	<@a :: "a">
	<@b :: "b">
```

- This is the same as in case of not using `this` for initialization.

## Compare using `this` and direct attribute usage

Code:

```-0
"EXAMPLE"
	variable "o" @tst

	class "tst"

		attribute "a" @VertexType
		attribute "y" @VertexType
		attribute "z" @VertexType

		method "doTest" ()
			a = "a"
			y = a
			z = this\a

	o = new @@tst[]
	o.doTest[]
```

Result:
```-0
""
	<@$Is :: @tst>
	<@a :: "a">
	<@y :: "">
	<@z :: @a:a>
```

Comment:
- `this` is essential on the right side of assignment. Probably in other places also.# Variables

	<@d :: "d">
	<@e :: "">
		"e"
	<@f :: "">

	<@b :: "b">
```

Comment:
- This is the same as in case of not using `this` for initialization.

## Compare using `this` and direct attribute usage

Code:

```-0

		attribute "y" @VertexType

			a = "a"
			y = a
			z = this\a

	o.doTest[]
```

Result:

""

	<@a :: "a">
	<@y :: "">
	<@z :: @a:a>
```

- `this` is essential on the right side of assignment. Probably in other places also.# Variables

## Compare using `this` and direct attribute usage

Code:

```-0
"EXAMPLE"
	variable "o" @tst

	class "tst"

		attribute "a" @VertexType
		attribute "y" @VertexType
		attribute "z" @VertexType

		method "doTest" ()
			a = "a"
			y = a
			z = this\a

	o = new @@tst[]
	o.doTest[]
```

Result:
```-0
""
	<@$Is :: @tst>
	<@a :: "a">
	<@y :: "">
	<@z :: @a:a>
```

Comment:
- `this` is essential on the right side of assignment. Probably in other places also.# Variables

	<@e :: "">
		"e"
	<@f :: "">
		"f"
```

Comment:
- `<+<` is not `AddRightEdgesIntoFirstLeftEdgeAndSetStoreForSubGraphAsIsInLeftVertex` as the `init` disapeared
- `<<<` is not `AddRightEdgesIntoFirstLeftEdgeAndSetStoreForSubGraphIncludingLinksAsIsInLeftVertex ` as the `init` disapeared

## Copied variable usage

Code:

```-0
"EXAMPLE"
	variable "a" @VertexType
	variable "b" @VertexType
	variable "c" @VertexType
	variable "d" @VertexType
	variable "e" @VertexType
	variable "f" @VertexType

	a = %"copy"
	b = %"copy"
	c = %"copy"
	d = %"copy"
	e = %"copy"
	f = %"copy"

	a = "a"
	b += "b"
	c +< "c"
	d <- "d"
	e <+< "e"
	f <<< "f"
```

Result:
```-0
""

	<@b :: "copy">
	<@b :: "b">
	<@c :: "copy">
		"c"
	<@d :: "d">
	<@e :: "">
		"e"
	<@f :: "">
		"f"
```

Comment:
- `<+<` is not `AddRightEdgesIntoFirstLeftEdgeAndSetStoreForSubGraphAsIsInLeftVertex` as the `copy` disapeared
- `<<<` is not `AddRightEdgesIntoFirstLeftEdgeAndSetStoreForSubGraphIncludingLinksAsIsInLeftVertex ` as the `copy` disapeared

			a = "init"
			b = "init"
			c = "init"
			d = "init"
			e = "init"
			f = "init"

			this\a = "a"
			this\b += "b"
			this\c +< "c"
			this\d <- "d"
			this\e <+< "e"
			this\f <<< "f"

	o = new @@tst[]
	o.doTest[]

		"e"

## Initialized attribute usage with `this` two times

Code:

```-0
"EXAMPLE"
	variable "o" @tst
	class "tst"
		attribute "a" @VertexType
		attribute "b" @VertexType
		attribute "c" @VertexType
		attribute "d" @VertexType
		attribute "e" @VertexType
		attribute "f" @VertexType
		method "doTest" ()
			this\a = "init"
			this\b = "init"
			this\c = "init"
			this\d = "init"
			this\e = "init"
			this\f = "init"
			this\a = "a"
			this\b += "b"
			this\c +< "c"
			this\d <- "d"
			this\e <+< "e"
			this\f <<< "f"
	o = new @@tst[]
	o.doTest[]
```

Result:
```-0
""
	<@$Is :: @tst>
	<@b :: "init">
	<@c :: "init">
		"c"
	<@d :: "d">
	<@e :: "">
		"e"
	<@f :: "">
		"f"
	<@a :: "a">
	<@b :: "b">
```

Comment:
- This is the same as in case of not using `this` for initialization.

## Compare using `this` and direct attribute usage

Code:

```-0
"EXAMPLE"
	variable "o" @tst

	class "tst"

		attribute "a" @VertexType
		attribute "y" @VertexType
		attribute "z" @VertexType

		method "doTest" ()
			a = "a"
			y = a
			z = this\a

	o = new @@tst[]
	o.doTest[]
```

Result:
```-0
""
	<@$Is :: @tst>
	<@a :: "a">
	<@y :: "">
	<@z :: @a:a>
```

Comment:
- `this` is essential on the right side of assignment. Probably in other places also.# Variables
